content.text: Tune high-impact vanilla Fallout 4 systems through one MCM: combat, character, economy, progression, perks, settlements, companions, and Survival mechanics.

content.help: Master switch for gameplay tweaks. Diagnostics can still run while disabled.
Controls House Rules log verbosity. Normal is recommended.

content.text: Each toggle: OFF mirrors vanilla Survival, ON applies the tweak.

content.help: ON re-enables the developer console while in Survival.
ON allows pause-menu saves and F5 quicksave in Survival.
ON allows queued vanilla auto-saves in Survival.
ON allows Pip-Boy fast travel in Survival. Fast-travel loads get targeted protection.
ON restores vanilla red-dot enemy markers on the compass.
ON restores vanilla undiscovered-location markers on the compass.
ON strips Survival's added weight from consumables (chems, stimpaks, food).
ON strips Survival's added weight from ammunition.
ON neutralizes Survival's -100 carry-weight ability for player and companions. Save and reload after toggling for the change to take effect on already-applied characters.
ON lets tgm and tim console cheats work in Survival.
ON lets you turn Survival back on after leaving it.

content.text: Toggles for the vanilla Survival hardcore-rule systems (food/water and sleep deprivation). Save and reload after toggling so the script picks up the new state immediately; otherwise the change takes effect on the next in-game tick (within a few in-game minutes).

content.help: ON disables the food and water need system. Stages no longer accumulate or apply. Survival difficulty stays active otherwise.
ON disables the sleep deprivation system. Sleep timer no longer accumulates and stages no longer apply. Survival difficulty stays active otherwise.
ON disables the Survival disease system. Risk events no longer roll and disease effects no longer apply. Survival difficulty stays active otherwise.
ON disables the Survival Adrenaline perk system. Kill streaks no longer build the Adrenaline rank, and the perk's damage bonus no longer applies. Survival difficulty stays active otherwise.
How much the food pool decreases each tick. Lower = needs accumulate slower. Vanilla 4.
How much the drink pool decreases each tick. Lower = needs accumulate slower. Vanilla 4.
How many in-game hours between sustenance ticks. Higher = needs accumulate slower. Vanilla 1.0.
Protected window after eating during which no further need accumulates. Vanilla 1.0.
Multiplier on sustenance accrual while asleep. Vanilla 0.25 (sleep slows need accumulation).
Extra in-game hours added to the sustenance tick during combat. Higher = combat makes you hungrier/thirstier faster. Vanilla 0.25.
Food pool value where the Peckish stage triggers. Lower (more negative) = later onset. Vanilla -24.
Food pool value where the Hungry stage triggers. Vanilla -48.
Food pool value where the Famished stage triggers. Vanilla -96.
Food pool value where the Ravenous stage triggers. Vanilla -144.
Food pool value where the Starving stage triggers. Vanilla -256.
Drink pool value where the Parched stage triggers. Vanilla -16.
Drink pool value where the Thirsty stage triggers. Vanilla -36.
Drink pool value where the Mildly Dehydrated stage triggers. Vanilla -72.
Drink pool value where the Dehydrated stage triggers. Vanilla -120.
Drink pool value where the Severely Dehydrated stage triggers. Vanilla -180.
In-game hours between sleep-deprivation checks. Higher = sleep stages progress slower. Vanilla 14.
Multiplier on sleep recovery when the Insomnia disease is active. Vanilla 0.5.
Minimum in-game hours of sleep needed to cure any sleep stage effects. Vanilla 2.
Hours of sleep delay imposed after consuming a caffeinated item. Vanilla 2.333.
Additional sleep-delay hours stacked for repeated caffeine use. Vanilla 7.
Multiplier on sleep-deprivation timer when Sleepiness disease is active. Lower = sleep stages progress faster while diseased. Vanilla 0.5.
Risk pool value above which a disease roll is made. Higher = diseases trigger less often. Vanilla 0.25.
In-game days after the last disease before another can trigger. Vanilla 1.0.
Passive disease-risk drain per cycle when no risk events fire. Vanilla -0.01 (negative drains pool).
Multiplier on the disease pool's current value. Vanilla 1.75.
Sustenance-cost multiplier when the Need More Food disease is active. Vanilla 0.5.
Disease-chance multiplier when Immunodeficiency is active. Vanilla 1.2.
Risk pool gain per cannibalism event. Vanilla 0.05.
Risk pool gain per chem use. Vanilla 0.07.
Risk pool gain from combat exposure to disease-carrying enemies. Vanilla 0.05.
Risk pool gain from high-risk food (raw, irradiated, etc.). Vanilla 0.12.
Risk pool gain from standard food. Vanilla 0.07.
Risk pool gain from being out in radioactive rain. Vanilla 0.03.
Risk pool gain from swimming. Vanilla 0.03.
Maximum tracked kill counter (max level x kills-per-level). Vanilla 50.
Kills needed to advance one Adrenaline perk rank. Vanilla 5.
Maximum Adrenaline perk rank. Vanilla 10.
Minimum in-game days between combat-disease risk events. Vanilla 0.1.
In-game hours between over-encumbrance damage ticks. Vanilla 24.

content.text: Implemented sliders multiply proven vanilla magnitudes. 1.00 = vanilla; see each setting for caveats.

content.help: Scales the HP restored per stimpak. Vanilla magnitude is 6 HP/sec over 5s.
Scales limb repair from stimpaks. Values above 1.0 are clamped by vanilla.
Scales the rads removed per RadAway. Vanilla magnitude is 60.
Scales the rad resistance granted by RadX. Vanilla magnitude is 100 over 180s.
Scales HP restored by food and drink. Tooltips may cache old values.
Scales the SPECIAL penalties at each hunger tier (Peckish through Starving).
Scales the SPECIAL penalties at each thirst tier (Thirsty through Severely Dehydrated).
Scales the AP-regen and SPECIAL penalties at each sleep tier (Tired through Incapacitated).

content.text: Per-difficulty multipliers applied as baseline * value. 1.00 = vanilla. Survival here is the current TSV difficulty.

How often eligible enemies become legendary. 1.00 = vanilla.
How often eligible enemies become legendary. 1.00 = vanilla.
Weights rarer legendary rolls after a legendary spawns. 1.00 = vanilla.
Weights rarer legendary rolls after a legendary spawns. 1.00 = vanilla.
Weights rarer legendary rolls after a legendary spawns. 1.00 = vanilla.
Weights rarer legendary rolls after a legendary spawns. 1.00 = vanilla.
Weights rarer legendary rolls after a legendary spawns. 1.00 = vanilla.
Weights rarer legendary rolls after a legendary spawns. 1.00 = vanilla.
Multiplies effect duration on Very Easy. 1.00 = vanilla.
Multiplies effect duration on Easy. 1.00 = vanilla.
Multiplies effect duration on Normal. 1.00 = vanilla.
Multiplies effect duration on Hard. 1.00 = vanilla.
Multiplies effect duration on Very Hard. 1.00 = vanilla.
Multiplies effect duration on Survival. 1.00 = vanilla (TSV baseline is much longer than Normal).
Multiplies effect magnitude on Very Easy. 1.00 = vanilla.
Multiplies effect magnitude on Easy. 1.00 = vanilla.
Multiplies effect magnitude on Normal. 1.00 = vanilla.
Multiplies effect magnitude on Hard. 1.00 = vanilla.
Multiplies effect magnitude on Very Hard. 1.00 = vanilla.
Multiplies effect magnitude on Survival. 1.00 = vanilla (TSV baseline is much smaller than Normal).

content.text: Action Points, sprint, carry weight, and health. Multiplier sliders show 1.00 = vanilla; Direct sliders use the vanilla default as neutral. Formula sliders (AP Pool, AP per Agility, Carry, Health per Endurance/Level) edit the actor-value formula. Your current derived value will refresh on the next level-up, equip change, fast-travel, or save reload.

content.help: Multiplies the base AP pool used by the engine's AP formula. Vanilla 1.00.
Multiplies the AP gain per Agility point. Vanilla 1.00.
Player AP restored per second before multipliers. Vanilla 6.
Combat AP regen multiplier. 0 disables combat regen. Vanilla 0.75.
Delay before AP regen after partial AP use. Vanilla 1.0.
Delay after sprinting to zero AP. Vanilla 2.0. Capped by AP Regen Delay Max.
Cap for AP regen delay. Vanilla 5.0.
Sprint AP drain base term. Vanilla 1.05.
Endurance term. More negative = Endurance reduces drain more. Vanilla -0.05.
Overall sprint AP drain multiplier. 1.00 = vanilla.
Multiplies the base carry capacity (the part not affected by Strength). Vanilla 1.00.
Multiplies carry capacity gained per Strength point. Vanilla 1.00.
Multiplies max health gained per Endurance point. Vanilla 1.00.
Multiplies max health gained per character level. Vanilla 1.00.
Player health regen rate. 1 = 1% max health. Vanilla 0.
Combat multiplier for passive health regen. 1 = full rate. Vanilla 0.

content.text: Raw damage factors and armor reduction exponents. Neutral values are vanilla; sliders move the engine baseline directly.

content.help: Raw radiation damage factor. Vanilla 0.15.
How strongly rad resistance reduces rads. Vanilla 0.365.
Raw physical damage factor. Stacks with Difficulty damage sliders.
How strongly physical armor reduces damage. Vanilla 0.365.
Raw energy damage factor. Stacks with Difficulty damage sliders.
How strongly energy resistance reduces damage. Vanilla 0.365.

content.text: Jetpack, fusion-core drain, and PA durability. Multiplier sliders show 1.00 = vanilla; Direct sliders show the vanilla default as their neutral value.

content.help: Fusion-core fuel consumed when a jetpack burst starts.
Continuous jetpack fuel drain.
Minimum core charge required to ignite. Vanilla 3.
Upward kick at ignition.
Continuous upward thrust.
Seconds before sustained jetpack mode. Vanilla 0.15.
Core drain per AP spent.
Core drain while running.
Core drain per jump. Vanilla 0.
Core drain per melee attack. Vanilla 0.
Core drain on hard landing. Vanilla 0.
Player PA condition damage multiplier. Vanilla 1. 0 = unbreakable.
NPC PA condition damage multiplier. Vanilla 3.

content.text: Vendor buy/sell pricing. Direct sliders show the vanilla default as their neutral value.

content.help: Higher = vendors charge more. Vanilla 2.0.
Higher = vendors pay less. Vanilla 3.5.
Cap on item value when buying. Vanilla 1.2.
Cap on item value when selling. Vanilla 0.8.
Minimum success chance the Charisma speech-check curve can fall to. Vanilla 0.5.
Maximum success chance the Charisma speech-check curve can reach. Vanilla 1.0.

content.text: Non-difficulty XP sources: crafting, lockpicking, mine disarm. Direct sliders show the vanilla default as their neutral value.

content.help: Flat XP per cook. Vanilla 1.

XP scaler from recipe value. Vanilla 0.15.
Flat XP per craft. Vanilla 2.
Cap on craft XP. Vanilla 50.
XP scaler from craft value. Vanilla 0.03.
Flat XP per build. Vanilla 2.
Cap on build XP. Vanilla 25.
XP scaler from build value. Vanilla 0.10.
XP per Apprentice pick. Vanilla 5.
XP per Adept pick. Vanilla 10.
XP per Expert pick. Vanilla 15.
XP per Master pick. Vanilla 20.
XP per disarmed mine/object. Vanilla 5.
XP per killed opponent. Vanilla 20.
XP per newly discovered map marker. Vanilla 20.
XP per discovered secret area. Vanilla 20.
Base XP scalar used by the level-up curve. Vanilla 200.
Per-level XP bump value. Vanilla 75.
Base XP for installing or removing a weapon/armor mod at a workbench. Vanilla 1.0.
Multiplier on Mod XP. Vanilla 0.03.
Engine penalty threshold for kills made just before the player's death. Vanilla 0.25.

content.text: Targeting range, time slowdown, and incoming damage while VATS is active. Direct sliders show the vanilla default as their neutral value.

content.help: Range where VATS can activate. 0 disables targeting. Vanilla 5000.
Lower = slower target selection. Vanilla 0.04.
Damage taken in VATS. Vanilla 0.10; 1 = no reduction.

content.text: Pickpocket, hacking, and lockpicking constants. Direct sliders show the vanilla default as their neutral value.

content.help: Lowest possible pickpocket chance. Vanilla 0%.
Highest possible pickpocket chance. Vanilla 90%.
Seconds before reverse-pickpocketed explosives detonate. Vanilla 2.
Fewest terminal password choices. Vanilla 6.
Most terminal password choices. Vanilla 20.
Base pick-break constant. Vanilla 0.05.
Base sweet-spot constant. Vanilla 0.20.
Higher = picks last longer. Vanilla 2.
Higher = picks last longer. Vanilla 2.
Higher = picks last longer. Vanilla 2.
Higher = picks last longer. Vanilla 2.
Sweet-spot width for Apprentice locks. Vanilla 80.
Sweet-spot width for Adept locks. Vanilla 60.
Sweet-spot width for Expert locks. Vanilla 40.
Sweet-spot width for Master locks. Vanilla 20.

content.text: Sneak attack and detection constants. Neutral values match vanilla.

content.help: Sneak attack damage multiplier for guns. Vanilla 2.
Sneak attack damage multiplier for fists and fist weapons. Vanilla 3.
Sneak attack damage multiplier for one-handed weapons. Vanilla 3.
Sneak attack damage multiplier for two-handed weapons. Vanilla 3.
Exterior sneak detection distance multiplier. Vanilla 1.25.
How much light affects detection while sneaking. Vanilla 0.33.
Maximum distance where enemies can spot you. Vanilla 4096.

content.text: Per-reaction affinity changes and event cooldowns. The five reaction sliders set how much affinity an event of that type adds (or subtracts) when a companion reacts to your action.

content.help: Affinity gained when a companion 'Loves' your action. Higher = bigger gain. Vanilla +35.
Affinity gained when a companion 'Likes' your action. Vanilla +15.
Affinity change for a neutral reaction. Vanilla 0 (no change).
Affinity lost when a companion 'Dislikes' your action. More negative = bigger loss. Vanilla -15.
Affinity lost when a companion 'Hates' your action. Vanilla -35.
Cooldown in game days for back-to-back reactions of the same type. Vanilla 0.
Cooldown in game days for short-tier reactions. Vanilla 0.05.
Cooldown in game days for medium-tier reactions. Vanilla 2.
Cooldown in game days for long-tier reactions. Vanilla 5.

content.text: Tier multipliers and trigger chances for combat perks. Direct sliders show the vanilla default as their neutral value.

content.help: Disarm chance from the Big Leagues / Iron Fist disarm rank. Vanilla 50.
Disarm chance when blocking with a melee weapon. Vanilla 50.
Stagger chance when blocking. Vanilla 5.
Recoil chance when blocking unarmed attacks. Vanilla 25.
Knockdown chance from the Rifleman / Gunslinger knockdown ranks. Vanilla 5.
Paralyze chance from the Rifleman / Gunslinger paralyze ranks. Vanilla 5.
Damage taken multiplier at Light Armor rank 1. Vanilla 1.5.
Damage taken multiplier at Light Armor rank 2. Vanilla 0.5.
Speed bonus at Light Armor rank 3. Vanilla 0.0.
Armor rating multiplier at Light Armor rank 4. Vanilla 1.5.
Minimum number of light armor pieces required to trigger the Master rating bonus. Vanilla 5.
Damage taken multiplier at Heavy Armor rank 1. Vanilla 1.5.
Damage taken multiplier at Heavy Armor rank 2. Vanilla 0.5.
Speed multiplier at Heavy Armor rank 3. Vanilla 0.5.
Speed multiplier at Heavy Armor rank 4. Vanilla 0.0.
Underwater gravity multiplier when wearing the heavy armor sink threshold. Vanilla 15.
Total armor weight at which heavy armor jump penalty triggers. Vanilla 35.
Total armor weight at which heavy armor sink (faster water descent) triggers. Vanilla 35.

content.text: Workshop GMSTs for build timers, repair cost, wire length, settler cap, and placement constraints. Settlement-surplus production caps are baked into script code, not game settings, so they aren't covered here.

content.help: Hard cap on settlers per settlement (before Charisma bonuses). Vanilla 10.
Seconds the workshop UI takes to register a build. Vanilla 0.1.
Seconds the workshop UI takes to deduct resources. Vanilla 0.4.
Component cost multiplier when repairing damaged objects. Vanilla 0.1.
Maximum reach of a single power wire connection. Vanilla 1100.
Minimum spacing between restricted build objects. Vanilla 32.
Radius around flora where restricted objects cannot be placed. Vanilla 50.
Radius around keyword-tagged objects where restricted builds are blocked. Vanilla 50.
Minimum spacing between automated turrets. Vanilla 70.
